localities.title: Localities
localities.typeDescription_bunari: Wells are deeper and narrow, structurally reinforced — stone-lined vertical excavations from which water is drawn using containers or pumps. To allow animals controlled access to drinking water, the water is transferred to stone basins or troughs nearby. Wells are sometimes covered or vaulted.
localities.typeDescription_skrapinski_bunari: Rock wells are entirely natural karst formations — depressions with near-vertical or steep walls where natural sediment deposits have made the base watertight, enabling water accumulation.
localities.typeDescription_gustrine: Cisterns (gustrine, šterne, čatrnje) are cavities or hollows between irregular natural rocks that people have adapted: sealing cracks with lime or other binding mixtures to retain water, sometimes also covering or enclosing them. As with wells, water is drawn from them.
localities.typeDescription_lokve: Ponds (lokve) are wider and shallower depressions, at least partly with gently sloping banks, offering water surfaces directly accessible to people and animals.
localities.typeDescription_kamenice: Stone basins (kamenice) are small natural or carved hollows in rock that collect rainwater. They served as a source of drinking water for people and animals along paths and pastures.

about.title: About the project
about.subtitle: Exploring and documenting the water sources of Slatine
about.introText: The "Slatinski Bunari" project is dedicated to researching, documenting, and presenting the water sources in Slatine on the island of Čiovo. Through a combination of fieldwork, digital technology, and education, we aim to preserve knowledge about traditional water sources that have served the local community for centuries.
about.introText2: This website allows visitors to explore different types of water localities through an interactive map, learn more about each source, and test their knowledge through an educational quiz.
about.goalText: The goal is to raise awareness about the importance of traditional water sources, encourage their preservation, and educate younger generations about the water heritage of their region.
about.schoolText: Slatine Elementary School is located in Slatine on the island of Čiovo, near the city of Trogir. The school is actively involved in numerous projects that connect education with the local community and cultural heritage.
about.schoolText2: Students and teachers of Slatine Elementary School are the driving force behind this project — from field research and data collection about water sources, to creating photographs and video recordings that document each locality.
about.stemSubtitle: Girls' aspirations towards educational and professional pathways in STEM - STeam
about.stemText: The "Slatinski Bunari" project was created as part of the STEM femme junior program, an initiative that encourages the participation of young people, especially girls, in STEM fields. The program provides mentorship support, resources, and a framework for developing research projects in schools across Croatia.
about.stemText2: The STEM femme junior project is led by the Institute for Social Research in Zagreb, with the support of partners and sponsors who enable the program's implementation in local communities.
about.sponsorsText: This project would not be possible without the support of our sponsors and partners who recognize the importance of education, cultural heritage preservation, and digital literacy.
about.partnerIDIZ: Institute for Social Research in Zagreb
